name: pytest-container

metadata.summary: Pytest fixtures for writing container based tests

documentation:
# Pytest container

```{image} https://github.com/dcermak/pytest_container/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg
:target: https://github.com/dcermak/pytest_container/actions/workflows/ci.yml
```

```{image} https://github.com/dcermak/pytest_container/actions/workflows/codeql-analysis.yml/badge.svg
:target: https://github.com/dcermak/pytest_container/actions/workflows/codeql-analysis.yml
```

```{image} https://codecov.io/gh/dcermak/pytest_container/branch/main/graph/badge.svg?token=D16Q2PGL67
:target: https://codecov.io/gh/dcermak/pytest_container
```

```{image} https://app.fossa.com/api/projects/git%2Bgithub.com%2Fdcermak%2Fpytest_container.svg?type=shield
:target: https://app.fossa.com/projects/git%2Bgithub.com%2Fdcermak%2Fpytest_container?ref=badge_shield
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/pytest-container
:alt: PyPI
:target: https://pypi.org/project/pytest-container/
```

Find the latest documentation on [dcermak.github.io/pytest_container](https://dcermak.github.io/pytest_container/).

`pytest_container` is a [pytest](https://pytest.org) plugin
to test container images via pytest fixtures and [testinfra](https://testinfra.readthedocs.io/en/latest/). It takes care of all the boring
tasks, like spinning up containers, finding free ports and cleaning up after
tests, and allows you to focus on implementing the actual tests.

The plugin automates the following tasks:

- pull, launch, and stop containers
- build containers using a `Dockerfile`
- wait for containers to become healthy before executing tests
- bind exposed container ports to free ports on the host
- mount volumes via temporary directories
- parallel test execution through pytest-xdist
- build dependent container images in the correct order
- run the same test on as many container images as necessary
- create, launch and destroy podman pods

`pytest_container` provides four fixtures that give you everything you need
for testing containers. Spinning up a container image can be as simple as
instantiating a `Container` and parametrizing a test function with the
`container` fixture:

```python
TW = Container(url="registry.opensuse.org/opensuse/tumbleweed:latest")

@pytest.mark.parametrize("container", [TW], indirect=["container"])
def test_etc_os_release_present(container: ContainerData):
    assert container.connection.file("/etc/os-release").exists
```

The fixture automatically pulls and spins up the container, stops it and removes
it after the test is completed. Your test function receives an instance of
`ContainerData` with the `ContainerData.connection` attribute. The
`ContainerData.connection` attribute is a [testinfra](https://testinfra.readthedocs.io/en/latest/) connection object. It can be
used to run basic tests inside the container itself. For example, you can check
whether files are present, packages are installed, etc.

## Use cases

1. Run functional tests on operating system container images
2. Verify your software on multiple operating systems

modules.name: pytest_container
documentation:
``pytest_container`` is a small pytest plugin to aid you in testing container
images or software in container images with pytest.

modules.name: pytest_container.logging
documentation: The logging module handles everything related to logging (unsurprisingly).

functions.name: pytest_container.logging.set_internal_logging_level
documentation: Set the verbosity of the internal logger to the specified level.

modules.name: pytest_container.helpers
documentation:
The helpers module contains various functions for adding & retrieving command
line flags from pytest and for automatically parametrizing tests using the
`auto_container*` fixtures.

functions.name: pytest_container.helpers.auto_container_parametrize
documentation:
Helper function to automatically parametrize the `auto_container_*`
fixtures.

Use it by adding the following code snippet to {file}`conftest.py`:

```python
from pytest_container import auto_container_parametrize

def pytest_generate_tests(metafunc):
    auto_container_parametrize(metafunc)
```

functions.name: pytest_container.helpers.add_extra_run_and_build_args_options
documentation:
Add the command line flags `--extra-run-args`, `--extra-build-args`
and `--extra-pod-create-args` to the pytest parser.

The parameters of these flags are used by the `*container*` and `pod*`
fixtures and can be retrieved via {py:func}`get_extra_run_args`,
{py:func}`get_extra_build_args` and {py:func}`get_extra_pod_create_args`
respectively.

functions.name: pytest_container.helpers.add_logging_level_options
documentation:
Add the command line parameter `--pytest-container-log-level` to the pytest
parser. The user can then configure the log level of this pytest plugin.

This function needs to be called in your {file}`conftest.py` in
`pytest_addoption`. To actually set the log level, you need to call
{py:func}`set_logging_level_from_cli_args` as well.

functions.name: pytest_container.helpers.set_logging_level_from_cli_args
documentation:
Sets the internal logging level of this plugin to the value supplied by the
cli argument `--pytest-container-log-level`.

This function has to be called before all tests get executed, but after the
parser option has been added. A good place is for example the
[pytest_configure](https://docs.pytest.org/en/latest/reference/reference.html#_pytest.hookspec.pytest_configure)
hook which has to be added to {file}`conftest.py`.

functions.name: pytest_container.helpers.get_extra_run_args
documentation:
Get any extra arguments for {command}`podman run` or {command}`docker run`
that were passed via the CLI flag `--extra-run-args`.

This requires that {py:func}`add_extra_run_and_build_args_options` was
called in {file}`conftest.py`.

functions.name: pytest_container.helpers.get_extra_build_args
documentation:
Get any extra arguments for {command}`buildah bud` or {command}`docker
build` that were passed via the CLI flag `--extra-build-args`.

This requires that {py:func}`add_extra_run_and_build_args_options` was
called in {file}`conftest.py`.

functions.name: pytest_container.helpers.get_extra_pod_create_args
documentation:
Get all extra arguments for {command}`podman pod create` that were passed
via the CLI flag `--extra-pod-create-args`.

This requires that {py:func}`add_extra_run_and_build_args_options` was
called in {file}`conftest.py`.

functions.name: pytest_container.helpers.get_always_pull_option
documentation:
Returns whether images should be always pulled before launching the
container or whether the container runtime can use the locally cached
image. This setting is controlled via the environment variable
`PULL_ALWAYS`. If the environment variable is unset, then the default is
`True`.

modules.name: pytest_container.build
documentation:
The build module contains helper classes for building from git repositories
via {py:class}`GitRepositoryBuild` and to perform multistage containerfile
builds via {py:class}`MultiStageBuild`.

classes.name: pytest_container.build.GitRepositoryBuild

methods.name: pytest_container.build.GitRepositoryBuild.repo_name
documentation: Name of the directory to which the repository will be checked out

methods.name: pytest_container.build.GitRepositoryBuild.clone_command
documentation: Command to clone the repository at the appropriate tag

methods.name: pytest_container.build.GitRepositoryBuild.test_command
documentation:
The full test command, including build_command and a cd into the
correct folder.

Test information storage for running builds using an external git
repository. It is a required parameter for the `container_git_clone` and
`host_git_clone` fixtures.

classes.name: pytest_container.build.MultiStageBuild

methods.name: pytest_container.build.MultiStageBuild.containerfile
documentation:
The rendered {file}`Containerfile` from the template supplied in
{py:attr}`containerfile_template`.

methods.name: pytest_container.build.MultiStageBuild.prepare_build
documentation:
Prepares the multistage build: it writes the rendered {file}`Containerfile`
into `tmp_path` and prepares all containers in {py:attr}`containers` in
the given `rootdir`. Optional additional build arguments can be passed
to the preparation of the containers

methods.name: pytest_container.build.MultiStageBuild.run_build_step
documentation:
Run the multistage build in the given `tmp_path` using the supplied
`runtime`. This function requires {py:meth}`prepare_build` to be run
beforehand.

Args:

: tmp_path: the path in which the build was prepared.
  runtime: the container runtime which will be used to perform the

  > build

  target: an optional target to which the build will be run, see the

  : upstream documentation
    \<<https://docs.docker.com/develop/develop-images/multistage-build/#stop-at-a-specific-build-stage>>\`\_
    for more information

Returns:

: Id of the final container that has been built

methods.name: pytest_container.build.MultiStageBuild.build
documentation:
Perform the complete multistage build to an optional target.

Args:
: tmp_path: temporary directory into which the {file}`Containerfile` is

  : written and where the build is performed. This value can be
    provided via the [tmp_path pytest fixture](https://docs.pytest.org/en/latest/how-to/tmp_path.html)

  rootdir_or_pytestconfig: root directory of the current test suite or

  : a [pytestconfig fixture](https://docs.pytest.org/en/latest/reference/reference.html?highlight=pytestconfig#std-fixture-pytestconfig)
    object. This value is used to prepare the containers in
    {py:attr}`containers`.

  runtime: the container runtime to be used to perform the build. It

  : can be retrieved using the
    {py:func}`pytest_container.plugin.container_runtime` fixture.

  target: an optional target to which the build will be run, see the

  : upstream documentation
    \<<https://docs.docker.com/develop/develop-images/multistage-build/#stop-at-a-specific-build-stage>>\`\_
    for more information. Note that **no** verification of the
    {file}`Containerfile` is performed prior to the
    build. I.e. specifying an invalid target will fail your build.

Returns:

: Id of the target container or of the last one (when no target was
  supplied) that was build

Helper class to perform multi-stage container builds using the
{py:class}`~pytest_container.container.Container` and
{py:class}`~pytest_container.container.DerivedContainer` classes.

This class is essentially just a very simple helper that will replace all
variables in {py:attr}`containerfile_template` with the correct container
ids, urls or names from the containers in {py:attr}`containers`.

For example the following class:

```python
MultiStageBuild(
    containers={
        "builder": Container(url="registry.opensuse.org/opensuse/busybox:latest"),
        "runner1": "docker.io/alpine",
    },
    containerfile_template=r'''FROM $builder as builder

FROM $runner1 as runner1
''',
)
```

would yield the following {file}`Containerfile`:

```Dockerfile
FROM registry.opensuse.org/opensuse/busybox:latest as builder

FROM docker.io/alpine as runner1
```

The resulting object can either be used to retrieve the rendered
{file}`Containerfile` or to build the containers:

```python
id_of_runner1 = MULTI_STAGE_BUILD.build(
    tmp_path, pytestconfig, container_runtime, "runner1"
)
```

Where `tmp_path` and `pytestconfig` are the pytest fixtures and
`container_runtime` is an instance of a child class of
{py:class}`~pytest_container.runtime.OciRuntimeBase`. For further details,
see {py:meth}`build`.

modules.name: pytest_container.plugin
documentation:
The plugin module contains all fixtures that are provided by
`pytest_container`.

functions.name: pytest_container.plugin.container_runtime
documentation:
pytest fixture that returns the currently selected container runtime
according to the rules outlined {ref}`here <runtime selection rules>`.

modules.name: pytest_container.pod
documentation: Module for managing podman pods.

functions.name: pytest_container.pod.infra_container_id_from_pod_inspect
documentation:
Given the output of {command}`podman pod inspect $id`, return the id of
the infra container.

functions.name: pytest_container.pod.pod_from_pytest_param
documentation:
Extracts the {py:class}`~pytest_container.pod.Pod` from a [pytest.param](https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/reference.html?#pytest.param) or just
returns the value directly, if it is a
{py:class}`~pytest_container.pod.Pod`.

classes.name: pytest_container.pod.Pod
documentation:
A pod is a collection of containers that share the same network and port
forwards. Currently only {command}`podman` supports creating pods.

**Caution**: port forwards of the individual containers are ignored and only
the port forwards in the pod class are taken into account!

classes.name: pytest_container.pod.PodData
documentation:
Class that is returned by the `pod` and `pod_per_test` fixtures. It
contains all necessary information about the created pod and the containers
running inside it.

classes.name: pytest_container.pod.PodLauncher

methods.name: pytest_container.pod.PodLauncher.from_pytestconfig
documentation:
Constructor of {py:class}`PodLauncher` that obtains the attributes
{py:attr}`rootdir`, {py:attr}`extra_build_args`,
{py:attr}`extra_run_args` and {py:attr}`extra_pod_create_args` from the
pytest configuration object.

methods.name: pytest_container.pod.PodLauncher.launch_pod
documentation:
Creates the actual pod, establishes the port bindings and launches
all containers in the pod.

methods.name: pytest_container.pod.PodLauncher.pod_data
documentation: Returns the {py:class}`PodData` corresponding to this podman pod.

A context manager that creates, starts and destroys a pod with all of its
containers.

modules.name: pytest_container.runtime
documentation:
This module contains the container runtime classes abstracting away the
implementation details of container runtimes like {command}`docker` or
{command}`podman`.

functions.name: pytest_container.runtime.get_selected_runtime
documentation:
Returns the container runtime that the user selected.

It defaults to podman and selects docker if podman & buildah are not
present. If podman and docker are both present, then docker is returned if
the environment variable `CONTAINER_RUNTIME` is set to `docker`.

If neither docker nor podman are available, then a ValueError is raised.

classes.name: pytest_container.runtime.ToParamMixin

methods.name: pytest_container.runtime.ToParamMixin.to_pytest_param
documentation: Convert this class into a ``pytest.param``

Mixin class that gives child classes the ability to convert themselves into
a pytest.param with self.__str__() as the default id and optional marks

classes.name: pytest_container.runtime.Version

methods.name: pytest_container.runtime.Version.parse
documentation: Parses a version string and returns a constructed Version from that.

Representation of a version of the form
`$major.$minor.$patch[-|+]$release build $build`.

This class supports basic comparison, e.g.:

```pycon
>>> Version(1, 0) > Version(0, 1)
True
>>> Version(1, 0) == Version(1, 0, 0)
True
>>> Version(5, 2, 6, "foobar") == Version(5, 2, 6)
False
```

Note that the patch and release fields are optional and that the release and
build are not taken into account for less or greater than comparisons only
for equality or inequality. I.e.:

```pycon
>>> Version(1, 0, release="16") > Version(1, 0)
False
>>> Version(1, 0, release="16") == Version(1, 0)
False
```

Additionally you can also pretty print it:

```pycon
>>> Version(0, 6)
0.6
>>> Version(0, 6, 1)
0.6.1
>>> Version(0, 6, 1, "asdf")
0.6.1 build asdf
```

classes.name: pytest_container.runtime.OciRuntimeABC

methods.name: pytest_container.runtime.OciRuntimeABC._runtime_error_message
documentation:
Returns a human readable error message why the runtime does not
function.

methods.name: pytest_container.runtime.OciRuntimeABC.get_container_health
documentation:
Inspects the running container with the supplied id and returns its current
health.

methods.name: pytest_container.runtime.OciRuntimeABC.version
documentation: The version of the container runtime.

methods.name: pytest_container.runtime.OciRuntimeABC.inspect_container
documentation:
Inspect the container with the provided `container_id` and return
the parsed output from the container runtime as an instance of
{py:class}`~pytest_container.inspect.ContainerInspect`.

methods.name: pytest_container.runtime.OciRuntimeABC.supports_healthcheck_inherit_from_base
documentation:
Indicates whether the container runtime supports that derived images
will inherit the healthcheck from the base image.
The abstract base class defining the interface of a container runtime.

classes.name: pytest_container.runtime.OciRuntimeBase

methods.name: pytest_container.runtime.OciRuntimeBase.get_image_id_from_iidfile
documentation:
Returns the image id/hash from the iidfile that has been created by
the container runtime to store the image id after a build.

methods.name: pytest_container.runtime.OciRuntimeBase.get_image_size
documentation:
Returns the container's size in bytes given an image id, a
{py:class}`~pytest_container.container.Container` or a
py:class:`~pytest_container.container.DerivedContainer`.

methods.name: pytest_container.runtime.OciRuntimeBase._get_image_entrypoint_cmd
documentation:
Inspect the container image with the given url or id and return its
`ENTRYPOINT` or `CMD` or `None` if no entrypoint or cmd has been
defined.
Base class of the Container Runtimes.

classes.name: pytest_container.runtime.PodmanRuntime

methods.name: pytest_container.runtime.PodmanRuntime.version
documentation: Returns the version of podman installed on the system
pragma pylint: disable=used-before-assignment

The container runtime using {command}`podman` for running containers and
{command}`buildah` for building containers.

classes.name: pytest_container.runtime.DockerRuntime

methods.name: pytest_container.runtime.DockerRuntime.version
documentation: Returns the version of docker installed on this system

The container runtime using {command}`docker` for building and running
containers.

modules.name: pytest_container.inspect
documentation:
This module contains the class definitions that represent the output of
{command}`$runtime inspect $ctr_id`.

classes.name: pytest_container.inspect.NetworkProtocol

methods.name: pytest_container.inspect.NetworkProtocol.SOCK_CONST
documentation:
Returns the appropriate socket type constant (`SOCK_STREAM` or
`SOCK_DGRAM`) for the current protocol.
Network protocols supporting port forwarding.

classes.name: pytest_container.inspect.PortForwarding

methods.name: pytest_container.inspect.PortForwarding.forward_cli_args
documentation:
Returns a list of command line arguments for the container launch
command to automatically expose this port forwarding.

Representation of a port forward from a container to the host.

To expose a port of a container automatically, create an instance of this
class, set the attribute {py:attr}`container_port` and optionally
{py:attr}`protocol` as well and pass it via the parameter
{py:attr}`~pytest_container.container.ContainerBase.forwarded_ports` to
either the {py:class}`pytest_container.container.Container` or
{py:class}`pytest_container.container.DerivedContainer`:

```pycon
>>> Container(url="my-webserver", forwarded_ports=[PortForwarding(container_port=8000)])
```

classes.name: pytest_container.inspect.ContainerInspectHealthCheck
documentation:
Dictionary created by loading the json output of {command}`podman inspect
$img_id | jq '.[0]["Healthcheck]` or {command}`docker inspect $img_id | jq
'.[0]["Config"]["Healthcheck]`.

classes.name: pytest_container.inspect.ContainerHealth
documentation:
Possible states of a container's health using the `HEALTHCHECK
<https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#healthcheck>`_ property
of a container image.

classes.name: pytest_container.inspect.HealthCheck

methods.name: pytest_container.inspect.HealthCheck.max_wait_time
documentation: The maximum time to wait until a container can become healthy

methods.name: pytest_container.inspect.HealthCheck.from_container_inspect
documentation:
Convert the json-loaded output of {command}`podman inspect $ctr` or
{command}`docker inspect $ctr` into a {py:class}`HealthCheck`.
The HEALTHCHECK of a container image.

classes.name: pytest_container.inspect.ContainerState
documentation:
State of the container, it is populated from the `State` attribute in
the inspect of a container.

classes.name: pytest_container.inspect.Config
documentation:
Container configuration obtained from the `Config` attribute in the
inspect of a container. It features the most useful attributes and those
that are common to both {command}`podman` and {command}`docker`.

classes.name: pytest_container.inspect.ContainerNetworkSettings
documentation: Network specific settings of a container.

classes.name: pytest_container.inspect.Mount
documentation: Base class for mount points

classes.name: pytest_container.inspect.BindMount
documentation: A bind mounted directory

classes.name: pytest_container.inspect.VolumeMount
documentation: A volume mount

classes.name: pytest_container.inspect.ContainerInspect
documentation:
Common subset of the information exposed via {command}`podman inspect`
and {command}`docker inspect`.

modules.name: pytest_container.container
documentation:
The container module contains all classes for abstracting the details of
launching containers away. These classes are used to parametrize test cases
using the fixtures provided by this plugin.

functions.name: pytest_container.container.lock_host_port_search
documentation: Generate a filelock for finding free ports on the host.

functions.name: pytest_container.container.create_host_port_port_forward
documentation:
Given a list of port_forwards, this function finds random free ports on
the host system to which the container ports can be bound and returns a new
list of appropriately configured
{py:class}`~pytest_container.inspect.PortForwarding` instances.

functions.name: pytest_container.container.get_volume_creator
documentation:
Returns the appropriate volume creation context manager for the given
volume.

functions.name: pytest_container.container.container_to_pytest_param
documentation:
Converts a subclass of {py:class}`~pytest_container.container.ContainerBase`
({py:class}`~pytest_container.container.Container` or
{py:class}`~pytest_container.container.DerivedContainer`) into a
[pytest.param](https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/reference.html?#pytest.param) with the
given marks and sets the id of the parameter to the pretty printed version
of the container (i.e. its
{py:attr}`~pytest_container.container.ContainerBase.url` or
{py:attr}`~pytest_container.container.ContainerBase.container_id`)

functions.name: pytest_container.container.container_and_marks_from_pytest_param
documentation:
Extracts the {py:class}`~pytest_container.container.Container` or
{py:class}`~pytest_container.container.DerivedContainer` and the
corresponding marks from a [pytest.param](https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/reference.html?#pytest.param) and
returns both.

If `param` is either a {py:class}`~pytest_container.container.Container`
or a {py:class}`~pytest_container.container.DerivedContainer`, then param is
returned directly and the second return value is `None`.

functions.name: pytest_container.container.container_from_pytest_param
documentation:
Extracts the {py:class}`~pytest_container.container.Container` or
{py:class}`~pytest_container.container.DerivedContainer` from a
[pytest.param](https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/reference.html?#pytest.param) or just
returns the value directly, if it is either a
{py:class}`~pytest_container.container.Container` or a
{py:class}`~pytest_container.container.DerivedContainer`.

classes.name: pytest_container.container.ImageFormat
documentation: Image formats supported by buildah.

classes.name: pytest_container.container.VolumeFlag
documentation: Supported flags for mounting container volumes.

classes.name: pytest_container.container.ContainerVolumeBase

methods.name: pytest_container.container.ContainerVolumeBase.cli_arg
documentation: Command line argument to mount this volume.
Base class for container volumes.

classes.name: pytest_container.container.ContainerVolume

methods.name: pytest_container.container.ContainerVolume.volume_id
documentation:
Unique ID of the volume. It is automatically set when the volume is
created by {py:class}`VolumeCreator`.

A container volume created by the container runtime for persisting files
outside of (ephemeral) containers.

classes.name: pytest_container.container.BindMount
documentation:
A volume mounted into a container from the host using bind mounts.

This class describes a bind mount of a host directory into a container. In
the most minimal configuration, all you need to specify is the path in the
container via {py:attr}`~ContainerVolumeBase.container_path`. The
`container*` fixtures will then create a temporary directory on the host
for you that will be used as the mount point. Alternatively, you can also
specify the path on the host yourself via {py:attr}`host_path`.

classes.name: pytest_container.container.VolumeCreator

methods.name: pytest_container.container.VolumeCreator.__enter__
documentation: Creates the container volume

methods.name: pytest_container.container.VolumeCreator.__exit__
documentation: Cleans up the container volume.

Context Manager to create and remove a {py:class}`ContainerVolume`.

This context manager creates a volume using the supplied
{py:attr}`container_runtime` When the `with` block is entered and removes
it once it is exited.

classes.name: pytest_container.container.BindMountCreator

methods.name: pytest_container.container.BindMountCreator.__enter__
documentation: Creates the temporary host path if necessary.

methods.name: pytest_container.container.BindMountCreator.__exit__
documentation: Cleans up the temporary host directory or the container volume.

Context Manager that creates temporary directories for bind mounts (if
necessary, i.e. when {py:attr}`BindMount.host_path` is `None`).

classes.name: pytest_container.container.EntrypointSelection
documentation: Choices how the entrypoint of a container is picked.

classes.name: pytest_container.container.ContainerBase

methods.name: pytest_container.container.ContainerBase._build_tag
documentation:
Internal build tag assigned to each immage, either the image url or
the container digest prefixed with `pytest_container:`.

methods.name: pytest_container.container.ContainerBase.local_image
documentation:
Returns true if this image has been build locally and has not been
pulled from a registry.

methods.name: pytest_container.container.ContainerBase.get_launch_cmd
documentation:
Returns the command to launch this container image.

Args:
: extra_run_args: optional list of arguments that are added to the

  : launch command directly after the `run -d`.

Returns:

: The command to launch the container image described by this class
  instance as a list of strings that can be fed directly to
  {py:class}`subprocess.Popen` as the `args` parameter.

methods.name: pytest_container.container.ContainerBase.filelock_filename
documentation:
Filename of a lockfile unique to the container image under test.

It is a hash of the properties of this class excluding all values that
are set after the container is launched. Thereby, this filename can be
used to acquire a lock blocking any action using this specific container
image across threads/processes.

Base class for defining containers to be tested. Not to be used directly,
instead use {py:class}`Container` or {py:class}`DerivedContainer`.

classes.name: pytest_container.container.ContainerBaseABC

methods.name: pytest_container.container.ContainerBaseABC.prepare_container
documentation: Prepares the container so that it can be launched.

methods.name: pytest_container.container.ContainerBaseABC.get_base
documentation:
Returns the Base of this Container Image. If the container has no
base, then `self` is returned.

methods.name: pytest_container.container.ContainerBaseABC.baseurl
documentation:
The registry url on which this container is based on, if one
exists. Otherwise `None` is returned.

Abstract base class defining the methods that must be implemented by the
classes fed to the `*container*` fixtures.

classes.name: pytest_container.container.Container

methods.name: pytest_container.container.Container.pull_container
documentation:
Pulls the container with the given url using the currently selected
container runtime

methods.name: pytest_container.container.Container.prepare_container
documentation: Prepares the container so that it can be launched.
This class stores information about the Container Image under test.

classes.name: pytest_container.container.DerivedContainer

methods.name: pytest_container.container.DerivedContainer.get_base
documentation: Return the base of this derived container.

Class for storing information about the Container Image under test, that
is build from a {file}`Containerfile`/{file}`Dockerfile` from a different
image (can be any image from a registry or an instance of
{py:class}`Container` or {py:class}`DerivedContainer`).

classes.name: pytest_container.container.ContainerData

methods.name: pytest_container.container.ContainerData.inspect
documentation:
Inspect the launched container and return the result of
{command}`$runtime inspect $ctr_id`.

methods.name: pytest_container.container.ContainerData.read_container_logs
documentation: Returns the logs from the running container.

Class returned by the `*container*` fixtures to the test function. It
contains information about the launched container and the testinfra
{py:attr}`connection` to the running container.

classes.name: pytest_container.container.ContainerLauncher

methods.name: pytest_container.container.ContainerLauncher.from_pytestconfig
documentation:
Constructor of {py:class}`ContainerLauncher` that obtains the
attributes {py:attr}`rootdir`, {py:attr}`extra_build_args` and
{py:attr}`extra_run_args` from the pytest configuration object.

methods.name: pytest_container.container.ContainerLauncher.launch_container
documentation:
This function performs the actual heavy lifting of launching the
container, creating all the volumes, port bindings, etc.pp.

methods.name: pytest_container.container.ContainerLauncher.container_data
documentation:
The {py:class}`ContainerData` instance corresponding to the running
container. This property is only valid after the context manager has
been "entered" via a `with` statement.

Helper context manager to setup, start and teardown a container including
all of its resources. It is used by the `*container*` fixtures.
